name: Bath Buns
recipeInstructions:
Bath buns, named after the English city where they originated, are made of a sweet egg dough.
NOTE: Original recipe used regular egg
NOTE: Original recipe used regular milk
NOTE: Original recipe used 1/4 Cup butter
Combine all dough ingredients except currants in bread pan according to your bread machine's instructions. Set for white or sweet bread and press Start. Add the currants after the first kneading or when the machine signals it's time to add fruit.
Grease a baking sheet. When dough is ready, remove from bread machine and gently punch down. Cut smaller batch into 8 pieces and larger batch into 12. Roll each piece into a ball. Place balls on baking sheet and slightly flatten each one. Cover loosely and let rise until doubled, about 45 minutes.
Preheat oven to 400°F. Make glaze by lightly beating egg and milk together. Brush tops of rolls with glaze and sprinkle with sugar. Bake until buns are golden brown, 15-20 minutes.
